story_prompt: create a lecture on javascript class

scenes.text: In this first scene, we introduce the concept of classes in JavaScript. A class is a blueprint for creating objects with predefined properties and methods. It simplifies the process of object creation, providing a clean, clear syntax.
scenes.image_prompt:
classDiagram
Class01 <|-- Class02
Class01 : +int field1
Class01 : +int field2
Class01 : +method1()
Class02 : +method2()

scenes.text: In the second scene, we discuss how to define a class in JavaScript using the 'class' keyword, followed by creating a constructor method to initialize object properties.
Next, we explore how to create methods within a JavaScript class. Methods are functions that belong to the class, allowing objects to perform actions.
scenes.image_prompt:
classDiagram
ClassA --|> methodA : functionality
ClassA : +function methodA(){...}
ClassA : +function methodB(param)

scenes.text: In this scene, the focus is on how inheritance works in JavaScript classes. Inheritance allows one class to extend another, inheriting its properties and methods.
The final scene covers practical usage and examples of JavaScript classes, demonstrating how to create instances and leverage polymorphism and encapsulation.
